Sources.Title: John D. Halamka — voluntarily released complete medical record, including the 20 June 2003 Quest draw
Personal Genome Project — public profile huC30901
John Halamka — The Personal Genome Project

TranscriptionNotes: John D. Halamka wrote that, as one of the Personal Genome Project's first ten participants, he contributed his entire medical record, phenotype, and genotype for public research.
The released record identifies him and places the complete blood count, chemistry panel, and cardio CRP under the same Quest order and 20 June 2003 08:45 collection.
AgeYears was calculated from the birth date and collection date shown in the voluntarily public record, rounded to two decimals; the birth date is not republished here.
Albumin was converted from g/dL to g/L by multiplying by 10.
Creatinine was converted from mg/dL to µmol/L by multiplying by 88.4.
Glucose was converted from mg/dL to mmol/L by multiplying by 0.0555.
